title: Topological Edge States from Molecular Chirality: A General Framework for Dimerized Dipolar Arrays
abstract: We establish a general theoretical framework for realizing topological edge states in dimerized arrays of chiral dipolar molecules and demonstrate that molecular handedness provides a natural and tunable route to SSH-like topology in an interacting one-dimensional setting. Starting from an effective spin-$\tfrac{1}{2}$ model generated by Stark-dressed chiral molecules, we introduce bond dimerization and show that the chirality-induced Dzyaloshinskii--Moriya interaction amplifies the effective hopping amplitudes and enlarges the bulk topological gap relative to an achiral chain of equivalent dipole strength. Using self-consistent mean-field theory with periodic- and open-boundary calculations, we map out the trivial, critical, and topological regimes through bulk spectra, complex-plane winding, and boundary-localized probability densities. A central result is that the two in-gap boundary modes carry \emph{opposite molecular chirality}: the left edge state localizes on a left-handed molecule and the right edge state on a right-handed molecule, a stereochemical labeling with no analogue in conventional SSH implementations. The two-leg ladder extension supports a richer four-band bulk structure and a rung-split edge sector whose robustness is characterized by a continuous sweep of the interchain coupling. All results are expressed in dimensionless units of the reference hopping scale $t_0$, making the framework directly applicable to any dipolar molecular platform -- from bialkali polar molecules at MHz coupling scales to future arrays of ultracold chiral polyatomic species. These findings establish dimerized chiral molecular arrays as a controllable and chirality-addressable platform for quasi-one-dimensional topological quantum matter.

bite.hook: Molecular chirality can naturally create topological edge states where opposite edges host opposite handedness.
tldr: They established a theoretical framework for topological edge states in dimerized arrays of chiral dipolar molecules.
They modeled Stark-dressed chiral molecules and analyzed the system using self-consistent mean-field theory.
They discovered that the two boundary modes carry opposite molecular chirality, localizing left-handed states on one side and right-handed on the other.
bite.whyItMatters: Topological states protect quantum information from noise, but creating them usually requires complex external setups. This research proves that the natural 'handedness' of molecules can do the heavy lifting, opening a new path to robust quantum materials.
bite.eli5: Imagine a line of spinning tops. If the tops are 'chiral' (meaning they have a left- or right-handed twist), they interact in a way that naturally locks the ends of the line in place. Remarkably, the left end of the line only accepts left-handed tops, and the right end only accepts right-handed ones.

glossary.shortDef: The geometric property of a molecule being non-superimposable on its mirror image (handedness).
glossary.eli5Def: The property of having a 'left' and 'right' version that can't be perfectly stacked on top of each other, just like your left and right hands.

glossary.shortDef: Robust, localized quantum states that exist at the boundaries of topological insulators.
glossary.eli5Def: Special, protected zones at the very edges of a material where quantum particles can hang out without being disturbed by outside noise.

glossary.shortDef: An antisymmetric quantum spin coupling that favors non-collinear spin alignments.
glossary.eli5Def: A magnetic force that prefers to twist neighboring spins perpendicular to each other rather than letting them line up straight.
